subject: ap-calculus-ab
title: Baby Bird dB/dt = (1/5)(100-B) — Weight Faster, Second Derivative Concavity & Separation of Variables
stimulus: The rate at which a baby bird gains weight is proportional to the difference between its adult weight and its current weight. At time $t = 0$, when the bird is first weighed, its weight is 20 grams. If $B(t)$ is the weight of the bird, in grams, at time $t$ days after it is first weighed, then $\frac{dB}{dt} = \frac{1}{5}(100 - B)$. Let $y = B(t)$ be the solution to the differential equation above with initial condition $B(0) = 20$.

parts.prompt: Is the bird gaining weight faster when it weighs 40 grams or when it weighs 70 grams? Explain your reasoning.
rubric_criteria: 1 pt: Evaluates $\frac{dB}{dt}$ at $B = 40$ and $B = 70$: $\frac{dB}{dt}\big|_{B=40} = \frac{1}{5}(60) = 12$ g/day and $\frac{dB}{dt}\big|_{B=70} = \frac{1}{5}(30) = 6$ g/day
1 pt: Correct answer with reason: the bird is gaining weight faster at 40 grams because $12 > 6$
parts.scoring_notes: From scoring key: $\frac{dB}{dt}|_{40} = 12 > 6 = \frac{dB}{dt}|_{70}$. Bird gains weight faster at $B = 40$.

parts.prompt: Find $\frac{d^2B}{dt^2}$ in terms of $B$. Use $\frac{d^2B}{dt^2}$ to explain why the graph of $B$ cannot resemble a graph with an inflection point (cannot be first concave up then concave down).
rubric_criteria: 1 pt: Explanation: since $B < 100$ for all $t \ge 0$, we have $100 - B > 0$, so $\frac{d^2B}{dt^2} = -\frac{1}{25}(100-B) < 0$; the graph of $B$ is always concave down, so it cannot have a portion that is concave up (ruling out the given graph shape)
parts.scoring_notes: From scoring key: $\frac{d^2B}{dt^2} = -\frac{1}{25}(100-B) < 0$ for $20 \le B < 100$. Graph is always concave down; cannot have a concave-up portion.

parts.prompt: Use separation of variables to find $y = B(t)$, the particular solution to the differential equation with initial condition $B(0) = 20$.
rubric_criteria: 1 pt: Uses initial condition $B(0) = 20$: $-\ln(80) = 0 + C \Rightarrow C = -\ln 80$; so $100 - B = 80e^{-t/5}$
parts.scoring_notes: From scoring key: $\int \frac{dB}{100-B} = \int \frac{dt}{5} \Rightarrow -\ln(100-B) = \frac{t}{5} + C$. Using $B(0)=20$: $C = -\ln 80$. $B(t) = 100 - 80e^{-t/5}$. 0/5 if no separation; max 2/5 if no constant.
